headline: Gig Workers Got SOCSO in 2025 — Retirement Floor Still Missing
context: The Gig Workers Bill 2025 brings ~1.2 million platform riders into SOCSO accident and disability coverage for the first time. EPF retirement contributions, sick leave, and broader informal-sector coverage remain outside the framework.

cards.big: Gig workers got SOCSO accident cover in 2025 — but still no retirement floor.
cards.sub: The Gig Workers Bill 2025 brings ~1.2 million riders into SOCSO accident protection. EPF retirement coverage and sick leave remain outside the framework.

cards.big: Voluntary opt-in schemes typically struggle to reach the workers most exposed to income shocks.
cards.sub: Malaysia's i-Saraan voluntary EPF programme covered 10.7% of self-employed workers in 2022, eight years after launch.

cards.big: Platform companies are not legally employers under the Employment Act.
cards.sub: The 2022 amendment excluded app-based engagement from the employer definition.

cards.big: Accident SOCSO is mandatory under the new bill; medical leave and retirement remain unprotected.
cards.sub: The first phase covers workplace injury and disability. Income smoothing during illness, and any retirement floor, fall outside the scheme.

cards.big: Accident coverage closes the cheapest gap. The expensive gaps — retirement, illness, income volatility — remain unaddressed.
Like fitting seatbelts but not building roads — the protection is real, the missing infrastructure is bigger.
cards.sub: Workplace accidents are the easiest risk to insure. Long-tail risks like outliving savings are the hardest, and the framework does not touch them.

cards.big: The Employment Act was written for factories. The 2025 bill is the first real adaptation. It is a floor, not a ceiling.
